tex:
The scatter diagram shows the value, in thousands of dollars, of ten paintings in 2005 and the value of the same paintings in 2025.
\newline

(a) The value of one of the paintings in 2025 is less than expected.
\newline

Draw a circle around the point that represents this painting.
\newline

(b) Another painting had a value of \$$75\,000$ in 2005 and \$$140\,000$ in 2025.
\newline

On the scatter diagram, plot this point.
\newline

(c) Write down the number of paintings with a value of less than \$$53\,000$ in 2005.
\newline

(d) What type of correlation is shown on the scatter diagram?

qtype: finding sum of prime numbers and identifying prime numbers within a range
tex:
(a) The sum of all the prime numbers less than 10 is equal to 17.
\newline

Find the sum of all the prime numbers less than 16.
\newline

(b) $x$ is an integer.
\newline

The sum of the prime numbers greater than 6 and less than $x$ is equal to 18.
\newline

Find a possible value for $x$.
\newline

content.content:
The sum of all the prime numbers less than 10 is equal to 17.
\newline

Find the sum of all the prime numbers less than 16.
\newline

$x$ is an integer.
\newline

The sum of the prime numbers greater than 6 and less than $x$ is equal to 18.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item The sum of all the prime numbers less than 10 is equal to 17.

\vgap

Find the sum of all the prime numbers less than 16.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item $x$ is an integer.

The sum of the prime numbers greater than 6 and less than $x$ is equal to 18.

\vgap

Find a possible value for $x$.

\vgap

\AnswerLine[$x = $][]
\Marks{1}

\end{parts}
